seo.title: E-Commerce AI Chatbot AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for ai chatbot AI agents in e-commerce. Covers FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS for payments.

content.intro: Deploying an AI chatbot in e-commerce introduces compounding risks across pricing accuracy, product claims, payment data handling, and consumer privacy — all governed by overlapping federal and state regulations. A misconfigured RAG pipeline or hallucinating language model can expose your business to FTC enforcement, CCPA/GDPR fines, and PCI DSS violations while simultaneously destroying customer trust. This checklist provides concrete, actionable controls to ensure your conversational agent operates safely, accurately, and in full regulatory compliance.

sections.heading: Product Claims & Pricing Accuracy
sections.description: Ensure the chatbot only surfaces substantiated product claims and real-time accurate pricing, preventing FTC violations and customer disputes caused by hallucinated or stale data.

items.label: Connect chatbot to live product catalog API
items.description: Integrate the RAG retrieval layer directly with your product catalog and pricing database so responses reflect real-time inventory, pricing, and availability — never cached or static data older than 15 minutes.

items.label: Implement hallucination guardrails for product specs
items.description: Deploy a post-generation verification step that cross-checks any product specification, feature claim, or compatibility statement against the retrieved source document before sending the response to the customer.

items.label: Block unsubstantiated superlative claims
items.description: Add a content filter that flags and rewrites responses containing unverifiable superlatives (e.g., 'best,' 'safest,' 'guaranteed to') unless those exact claims appear in the verified product data source.

items.label: Validate discount and promotional pricing before display
items.description: Before the chatbot quotes a sale price or promo code, query the promotions engine in real time to confirm the offer is active, applicable to the customer's cart, and not expired.

items.label: Log all pricing statements for audit trail
items.description: Capture every chatbot message containing a price, discount percentage, or total estimate in an immutable audit log with timestamp, session ID, and the data source version used to generate it.

items.label: Display price disclaimer for dynamic or regional pricing
items.description: When the chatbot provides pricing that may vary by region, membership tier, or tax jurisdiction, append a standardized disclaimer directing the customer to confirm final price at checkout.

items.label: Test chatbot responses against known pricing edge cases monthly
items.description: Run a regression test suite of at least 50 pricing-related prompts — including bundle deals, clearance items, and out-of-stock scenarios — each month to catch retrieval or generation errors before customers do.

sections.heading: Privacy, PII Protection & Data Governance
sections.description: Protect customer data collected during multi-turn conversations in compliance with CCPA and GDPR, ensuring PII is never stored, logged, or used beyond its stated purpose.

items.label: Scrub PII from conversation memory before storage
items.description: Apply a PII detection and redaction pipeline (covering names, email addresses, phone numbers, shipping addresses, and payment references) to all conversation turns before they are written to any persistent memory or logging store.

items.label: Enforce session-scoped memory with automatic expiry
items.description: Configure multi-turn memory to be session-scoped only, with automatic deletion at session end or after 30 minutes of inactivity, unless the customer has explicitly opted into persistent personalization.

items.label: Present chatbot data collection disclosure at session start
items.description: Display a clear, plain-language notice at the start of every chat session explaining what data is collected, how it is used, and how customers can request deletion — satisfying CCPA and GDPR transparency requirements.

items.label: Implement opt-out mechanism within the chat interface
items.description: Provide a visible, one-click option within the chatbot UI for customers to opt out of data collection or request deletion of their conversation history, with the request routed to your DSAR (Data Subject Access Request) workflow.

items.label: Prohibit chatbot from requesting full payment card data
items.description: Configure intent classification to detect and block any conversation flow that would cause the chatbot to solicit, repeat, or store full credit card numbers, CVVs, or expiration dates — routing payment capture exclusively to PCI-compliant checkout flows.

items.label: Conduct Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) before launch
items.description: Complete a formal DPIA documenting all personal data flows through the chatbot system, identifying risks and mitigations, and obtaining sign-off from your DPO or legal counsel before going live.

items.label: Restrict third-party LLM provider data retention
items.description: Confirm via contract and technical configuration that your LLM API provider does not retain conversation data for model training, and that data processing agreements (DPAs) are in place for GDPR compliance.

items.label: Anonymize conversation logs used for chatbot improvement
items.description: Before using any conversation logs for fine-tuning, evaluation, or analytics, apply k-anonymization or differential privacy techniques and obtain explicit consent where required by applicable law.

sections.heading: Return, Refund & Policy Representation
sections.description: Ensure the chatbot accurately represents your return, refund, and warranty policies at all times, preventing disputes, chargebacks, and FTC violations from misrepresented terms.

items.label: Sync chatbot policy knowledge base with live policy documents
items.description: Connect the RAG retrieval index to your official return, refund, and warranty policy documents, with an automated re-indexing trigger that fires within 1 hour of any policy update going live on your website.

items.label: Block chatbot from improvising policy exceptions
items.description: Implement a guardrail that prevents the chatbot from offering policy exceptions, extended return windows, or goodwill credits not explicitly authorized in the retrieved policy document — escalating such requests to a human agent instead.

items.label: Include direct link to full policy in every policy-related response
items.description: Whenever the chatbot discusses return eligibility, refund timelines, or warranty coverage, append a hyperlink to the full, current policy page so customers can verify the complete terms independently.

items.label: Test policy responses against all product category exceptions
items.description: Maintain a test suite covering policy edge cases by product category (e.g., final sale items, digital downloads, perishables, hazmat) and run it after every policy update to verify the chatbot handles each exception correctly.

items.label: Escalate refund disputes to human agents with full context
items.description: When a customer disputes a refund decision made or communicated by the chatbot, automatically escalate to a human agent and pass the full conversation transcript and order details to prevent the customer from having to repeat themselves.

items.label: Audit chatbot policy responses against resolved dispute outcomes quarterly
items.description: Each quarter, sample 50 resolved refund or return disputes and trace them back to the chatbot conversation that preceded the dispute to identify systematic misrepresentation patterns in the model's responses.

items.label: Disclose chatbot limitations on binding commitments
items.description: Include a standard disclosure in the chatbot's initial greeting and in any policy-related response clarifying that chatbot responses are informational and that only written confirmation from customer service constitutes a binding commitment.

sections.heading: Accessibility, Safety Filtering & Operational Readiness
sections.description: Ensure the chatbot meets ADA accessibility standards, applies robust content safety filters, and has the operational monitoring and human escalation paths required for a high-risk production deployment.

items.label: Achieve WCAG 2.1 AA compliance for the chat widget
items.description: Audit the chatbot UI widget for keyboard navigability, screen reader compatibility (ARIA labels), sufficient color contrast ratios, and focus management — remediating all Level A and AA failures before launch to satisfy ADA requirements.

items.label: Implement multi-layer content safety filtering
items.description: Apply both a pre-generation input filter (blocking prompt injection, jailbreak attempts, and abusive content) and a post-generation output filter (blocking harmful, discriminatory, or legally sensitive content) before any response reaches the customer.

items.label: Configure human escalation triggers with defined SLA
items.description: Define explicit escalation triggers — including customer frustration signals, refund disputes, account security concerns, and three consecutive low-confidence responses — and guarantee a human agent response within a documented SLA (e.g., 2 minutes for live chat, 4 hours for email follow-up).

items.label: Disclose AI identity at session start and on request
items.description: Ensure the chatbot clearly identifies itself as an AI assistant at the beginning of every conversation and responds honestly when a customer directly asks whether they are speaking to a human — in compliance with FTC disclosure guidance.

items.label: Establish real-time monitoring dashboard for response quality
items.description: Deploy a monitoring dashboard tracking hallucination rate, escalation rate, policy misrepresentation flags, PII detection events, and customer satisfaction scores — with alerting thresholds that trigger an on-call review when any metric exceeds baseline by 20%.

items.label: Conduct adversarial red-team testing before launch
items.description: Perform structured red-team testing with at least 200 adversarial prompts targeting prompt injection, policy manipulation, PII extraction, and false pricing claims — documenting all findings and remediating critical issues before go-live.

items.label: Define and test chatbot rollback procedure
items.description: Document a rollback procedure that can revert the chatbot to the previous model version or disable it entirely within 15 minutes of a critical failure, and test this procedure in a staging environment at least once per quarter.

items.label: Provide alternative non-chatbot support path for all users
items.description: Ensure that customers who cannot or choose not to use the AI chatbot have immediate access to an alternative support channel (phone, email, or live human chat) that is equally prominent in the UI — satisfying both ADA and general accessibility best practices.

regulatoryNotes.heading: Regulatory Considerations
notes: FTC Advertising Guidelines require that all product claims made by the chatbot — including AI-generated responses — be truthful, non-deceptive, and substantiated by reliable evidence. The FTC's 2023 guidance on AI explicitly extends these obligations to automated systems, meaning your chatbot's product descriptions and promotional claims carry the same legal weight as human advertising copy.

CCPA and GDPR impose strict requirements on the collection, processing, and retention of personal data gathered during chatbot conversations. Under CCPA, California residents have the right to know, delete, and opt out of the sale of their personal information — rights that must be honored within 45 days of a verifiable request. GDPR additionally requires a lawful basis for processing (typically consent or legitimate interest) and mandates Data Processing Agreements with all third-party LLM providers handling EU resident data.

PCI DSS v4.0 prohibits storing sensitive authentication data (full card numbers, CVVs, PINs) in any system component, including chatbot conversation logs and memory stores. If your chatbot operates within the cardholder data environment or can influence payment flows, it must be included in your annual PCI DSS scope assessment and penetration testing program — and any third-party AI provider used must demonstrate PCI DSS compliance or be isolated from cardholder data entirely.

The Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and Section 508 require that customer-facing digital interfaces, including AI chatbots, be accessible to users with disabilities. Courts have increasingly applied ADA Title III to e-commerce websites and their embedded tools, with chatbot inaccessibility cited in multiple settlements. Compliance requires WCAG 2.1 AA conformance, regular accessibility audits, and a documented remediation process for reported barriers.

cta.heading: Automate Your E-Commerce Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your e-commerce AI chatbot against this checklist — automatically detecting pricing hallucinations, PII leakage, policy misrepresentations, and safety violations before they reach your customers.
